title: Carnot Efficiency
content: The maximum thermal efficiency of any heat engine operating between two temperature reservoirs is the Carnot efficiency: η_max = 1 − T_L / T_H.
extended_content: T_H is the absolute temperature of the hot reservoir and T_L is the absolute temperature of the cold reservoir (both in Kelvin). No real engine can exceed this limit; practical engines are further limited by irreversibilities such as friction, heat transfer across finite temperature differences, and internal leakage.
source: Cengel & Boles, Thermodynamics: An Engineering Approach, 8th ed.
